posts.content:
I just read this article about a UX design sprint and honestly it was really helpful for understanding how the process actually works in the real world.
The biggest thing I took away is that designers don't just pick one idea and run with it. They made three totally different versions of the same problem and tested which one felt right. That's something I will do in my own practice.

The psychology behind direction one was so interesting to me, of course. Making the recipe photos slightly blurry so they feel just out of reach? That's smart. It's not just a visual choice, it's intentional. It makes the user WANT to unlock it.

I also didn't know Figma could remove objects from photos or turn a hand drawn sketch into a vector. Like that feels like it changes everything about the workflow. I've been using Figma pretty basically so knowing it can do that is fantastic.

The main thing I'm bringing into my learning is that good UX is really about guiding someone toward an action without them feeling pushed. The visual stuff supports that goal, it doesn't replace it. .

ai.title: I built a full short film pipeline with Runway + DaVinci Resolve + Claude for scripting — here's every step
ai.summary: Complete breakdown of a 3-minute AI short film production pipeline. Covers prompt iteration for shot consistency, temporal coherence tricks, and how Claude was used to generate shot lists and color grading notes.
ai.why_important: One of the first detailed process breakdowns showing AI video gen integrated into a real post-production pipeline with professional NLE.
ai.learnings: Shot consistency technique using seed locking + style reference frames; Claude-assisted color script generation.

author.name: Sota Mikami
author.headline: Product Designer & Product Manager | From Concept to Launch: Creating Impactful Digital Products

posts.content:
I got tired of the tool-hopping: Figma for overviews, a separate prototype for interaction, another doc for specs, yet another for QA.

So I built a design environment around Claude Code where everything lives under one URL — interactive prototype, screen flow map, spec, and QA cases.

I shared the process in Japanese and it resonated with 293k+ views on X. Now publishing the English version for a broader conversation.

If you're experimenting with AI in your design/PM workflow, I'd love to connect.

ai.title: ComfyUI + 3DGS: my workflow for turning phone scans into stylized game assets in under 10 minutes
ai.summary: Detailed node-by-node ComfyUI workflow for processing phone LiDAR scans through 3DGS, then applying style transfer for game-ready assets. Includes downloadable workflow JSON.
ai.why_important: Bridges the gap between casual 3D capture and production-ready game assets using an entirely open-source pipeline.
ai.learnings: ComfyUI node chain for 3DGS post-processing; style transfer technique that preserves geometric detail.

author.name: Hiten Nainaney
author.headline: Head of Design - AI Factory | Co-Founder @Orikami Lab | Government Innovation Consultant

posts.content:
I'm done hiring designers who think great design takes weeks.

We're not doing traditional design workflows anymore. No more three-week user flow exercises. No more Figma-first thinking. No more 'let me run another round of research' before a single screen exists.

I need design builders. People who wake up, identify a problem, use AI to map the experience, generate the UI, and have a functional prototype in someone's hands by the end of the week.

I took an idea from zero to a live app in TestFlight in 7.5 hours. That's not a flex. That's the new baseline.

If you're still leading with Figma and calling it innovation, this isn't the role for you.

But if you're an AI-native designer who can move fast, think sharp, and build real things, reach out. I'm hiring and I'm looking for you specifically.

DM me.

ai.title: How I use Figma AI + Claude to go from user research notes to interactive prototype in one afternoon
ai.summary: Step-by-step process: research synthesis with Claude → information architecture → Figma AI layout generation → v0 for interactive prototype. Shows real before/after of each stage.
ai.why_important: Demonstrates a complete AI-augmented design workflow that maintains design judgment while dramatically reducing mechanical work.
ai.learnings: Prompt structure for Claude research synthesis; Figma AI layout generation best practices; when to switch from AI to manual design.

author.name: Ajay Wadne
author.headline: UI/UX Design Team Lead | 14+ yrs crafting engaging interfaces | Front-End Dev (HTML, CSS, React)

posts.content:
Big Update for UI/UX Designers!

Figma just introduced a powerful new capability — you can now bring UI work created in Claude Code directly into Figma as fully editable design frames.

Not static files.
Not screenshots.
But real, structured, editable frames.

What this unlocks:

• Convert AI-generated UI into organized Figma designs
• View complete multi-page flows on your canvas
• Rapidly experiment and reimagine user experiences
• Use Figma MCP to push updated designs back into Claude Code

This creates a seamless two-way workflow between design and AI development — helping teams prototype, iterate, and ship faster than ever.

We’re entering a phase where design and code evolve together in real time.

🔗 Learn more about the update here:
https://lnkd.in/dNDkpcmg

ai.title: After Effects + Runway + Rive: building a hybrid motion system for product launches
ai.summary: Shows how to combine traditional After Effects animation with AI-generated backgrounds from Runway, then export interactive components via Rive. Includes project file breakdown.
ai.why_important: Practical hybrid workflow that doesn't replace traditional motion design but augments it — the approach most studios will actually adopt.
ai.learnings: Compositing AI-generated elements with hand-animated motion; Rive export optimization for web; timeline synchronization technique.

author.name: Gabriela Sierra
author.headline: Product Designer | Merging Aesthetic UI with Thoughtful UX | Figma • Design Systems • Accessibility

posts.content:
AI isn’t replacing designers 🙅🏻‍♀️

But it is changing how we work 💡

Lately, I’ve been experimenting with integrating AI into my design workflow — using tools like ChatGPT, Claude, Figma Make, and Replit to explore ideas faster and prototype more quickly.

In this article, I share how I’m approaching AI as a thought partner rather than a replacement in the design process.

Because while AI can generate interfaces, designers generate understanding.

Would love to hear how other designers are using AI in their workflows.

ai.title: text-to-3D is finally usable: my honest assessment after testing every major tool on real client projects
ai.summary: Comparative review of text-to-3D tools tested on actual client briefs. Covers mesh quality, texture fidelity, and what still requires manual cleanup. Brutally honest about limitations.
ai.why_important: Goes beyond hype to show exactly where text-to-3D fits in a professional 3D pipeline and where it still falls short.
ai.learnings: Which text-to-3D tool works best for which asset type; manual cleanup workflow for AI-generated meshes; client communication strategy for AI-assisted work.

author.name: Anamika S.
author.headline: Lead UX Researcher, Growth @ Marvalero Inc. | Building 0–1 Digital Identities

posts.content:
Hey LinkedIn Network, I’m looking for my next challenge! 🚀

I recently wrapped up leading a product from 0 → 1 at Marvalero Inc., where I spent 6 months building digital environments and shipping a dual platform experience from scratch.

What I bring to the table:

💻  I love turning "too many ideas" and "too many flows" into one clear, story-driven product. 

✨  I bridge the gap between deep research and high-fidelity Figma prototypes. Turning "what if" into "here it is"! 

🔐  I also leverage pattern recognition and automation (Claude/Gemini/GPT) for Automated QA and Workflow Optimization. I use AI to move faster while keeping the final design decisions human and grounded.

✏️ Portfolio: helloanamika.com 
( the desktop version is much more fun to scroll)

If you're hiring—or know someone who is—I would love to chat! 📩 

#UXDesign #ProductDesign #Hiring #DesignTech #0to1 #UXJobs

ai.title: Shot consistency in AI video: the techniques that actually work after 200+ generation tests
ai.summary: Systematic comparison of consistency techniques across AI video platforms. Tests character locking, style reference, seed manipulation, and LoRA training. Quantifies success rates.
ai.why_important: Shot consistency is the #1 blocker for professional AI video adoption. This is the most rigorous comparison available.
ai.learnings: Seed + reference frame technique for Kling; LoRA training pipeline for character consistency; when to give up on gen AI and use traditional VFX.

posts.content:
We’re hiring: Senior Product Designer (Enterprise SaaS | AI Workflows) 
📍  Pune

I’m currently looking for a Senior Product Designer to join QAD, a global SaaS company transforming manufacturing and supply chains.

We’re looking for someone who enjoys working on:
✨ Complex enterprise products (ERP / B2B SaaS)
✨ End-to-end user journeys (research → workflows → prototypes)
✨ AI-driven features & intelligent user experiences
✨ Solving real-world problems at scale

🔍 Ideal background:

• 4-8 years in Product / UX Design
• Experience with enterprise or workflow-heavy products
• Strong in research, interaction design & storytelling
• Hands-on with tools like Figma

If this sounds like you or someone in your network. I’d love to connect!

ai.title: design-tokens-ai: open-source tool that extracts design tokens from any Figma file and generates theme code
ai.summary: Open-source CLI tool that reads a Figma file, extracts all design tokens (colors, spacing, typography), and generates Tailwind config, CSS variables, or Swift/Kotlin theme files.
ai.why_important: Solves the design-to-dev token handoff problem that every team faces. Production-ready with 1.8K GitHub stars.
ai.learnings: Figma API token extraction patterns; multi-platform code generation architecture; how to maintain token parity across platforms.

posts.content:
“Prototype, not PowerPoint.”
In 2012, that line felt almost rebellious. Today, the tools have finally caught up with the idea.
 
With AI embedded across our workflows—from Figma to Miro to Claude—the distance between concept and working thing has collapsed. Prototypes can now carry real data, real logic, even AI-native interactions from day one.
 
Great news for speed.
Slightly dangerous for thinking.
 
Because when building becomes effortless, the real differentiator isn’t the tool—it’s the discipline behind it.
 
Our CXO Ralf Gehrig unpacks what’s changed, what hasn’t, and why rigor still beats velocity in product design.
 
Read the full piece 👇 
https://lnkd.in/dgWG4hSY
 
#ProductDesign #CX #AI #Prototyping #DesignLeadership

ai.title: I replaced 60% of my After Effects work with Rive + AI — here's what I gained and what I lost
ai.summary: Honest account of transitioning from After Effects to Rive for UI animation work, using Midjourney for asset generation. Covers what types of motion work well in each tool.
ai.why_important: Rare honest assessment of tool migration costs and benefits from someone with 8+ years of AE experience.
ai.learnings: Decision framework for AE vs Rive per project type; Midjourney prompt techniques for motion-ready assets; interactive animation patterns that don't work in traditional video export.

posts.content:
We're hiring an AI Prototype Engineer at MS Direct Group – and this one's for the builders.

You know the feeling: you open your editor, connect an API, build a first version, show it, break it, fix it, ship it. That's what we need.

MS Direct runs fulfillment, customer service, and logistics for leading Swiss and European brands. Now we're building an AI Office to bring intelligence into every layer of what we do. And we need someone who can turn use cases into working prototypes – fast, very fast.

This role is about building AI agents. Real ones. Agents that classify, route, extract, summarize, and act. You'll work with LLM APIs, design multi-step agent workflows, build RAG systems, and test different models against each other – all in a secure sandbox before anything touches production.

What matters to us:
→ You've built agents – not just read about them
→ You understand prompt engineering as a craft, not a buzzword
→ You think in iterations: build, test, learn, repeat
→ Vibe coding with Claude, Cursor, Figma or Copilot is how you work – but you understand what you build

Start at 50% (perfect alongside studies), grow to 80–100%.
St. Gallen · Hybrid · Inhouse, not outsourced.

We're not buying AI off the shelf. We're building it. Come build with us.

#AI #AIAgents #Hiring #LLM #PromptEngineering #Switzerland #Startup

ai.title: gaussian-splatting-editor: open-source web viewer and editor for 3DGS scenes with AI-powered inpainting
ai.summary: Web-based 3DGS scene editor that runs in-browser with WebGPU. Supports direct manipulation, AI inpainting for scene editing, and export to standard 3D formats.
ai.why_important: Makes 3D Gaussian Splatting editable for the first time in a browser — huge for accessibility of this technology to non-engineers.
ai.learnings: WebGPU rendering pipeline for 3DGS; AI inpainting integration for scene modification; browser-based 3D editing architecture.

author.name: Ghulam Qadir
author.headline: Senior Product & UX Designer | SaaS Dashboards · Design Systems · AI Interfaces · Landing Pages | Ex-Broadcom, Symantec | Open to Remote & Freelance

posts.content:
Claude 𝗖𝗼𝗱𝗲 𝗷𝘂𝘀𝘁 𝗵𝗶𝘁 $𝟮.𝟱 𝗕𝗜𝗟𝗟𝗜𝗢𝗡 𝗶𝗻 𝗿𝗲𝘃𝗲𝗻𝘂𝗲 - 𝗶𝗻 𝗷𝘂𝘀𝘁 𝟵 𝗺𝗼𝗻𝘁𝗵𝘀.

4% of all GitHub commits are now written by AI. By year-end 2026? That number could hit 20%.

Meanwhile, Figma launched an AI that generates full UI from text prompts.

𝗦𝗼 𝗜 𝗵𝗮𝘃𝗲 𝘁𝗼 𝗮𝘀𝗸:
Is the traditional UX design process — research → wireframe → design → handoff → develop — becoming a relic?

𝗛𝗲𝗿𝗲'𝘀 𝘄𝗵𝗮𝘁 𝗜 𝗳𝗼𝘂𝗻𝗱 𝗮𝗳𝘁𝗲𝗿 𝗱𝗲𝗲𝗽 𝗿𝗲𝘀𝗲𝗮𝗿𝗰𝗵:
→ Teams report 3-5x faster feature delivery with AI tools
→ One person can now build what took 5-person teams
→ 85% of developers use AI coding tools daily
→ The new workflow is: prompt → prototype → deploy

𝗕𝘂𝘁 𝗵𝗲𝗿𝗲'𝘀 𝘁𝗵𝗲 𝘁𝘄𝗶𝘀𝘁:
If everyone uses the same AI, everything starts to look the same.
The designers who win in 2026 aren't the best at Figma.

They're the best at:
Defining problems worth solving
Setting constraints that guide AI
Maintaining human judgment

I wrote a full analysis on what this means for product designers, CEOs, & startup founders.

Read below ↓

#UXDesign #ProductDesign #AI #FutureOfWork #StartupFounders
 #AIDevelopment #DesignSystems #TechTrends

ai.title: The real cost of AI-generated UI: what breaks when you skip the design thinking
ai.summary: Critical analysis of AI-generated interfaces from a design leadership perspective. Shows specific examples where AI output looked polished but failed usability testing, and proposes a hybrid QA framework.
ai.why_important: Counter-narrative to 'AI replaces designers' — provides the quality framework for when and how to use AI generation responsibly.
ai.learnings: Usability testing framework for AI-generated UI; specific failure patterns to watch for; hybrid human-AI design review process.
